mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months with rising PAYEMS, or mean return in those months is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt-service costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment combined with a low federal funds rate indicates a non-inflationary expansion that lifts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the joint configuration produces a stronger effect than either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: A linear model with the UNRATE*FEDFUNDS interaction term does not outperform a simple additive model in predicting 20-day returns, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales and housing starts validates broad-based domestic demand strength, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the signal requires both indicators to move together.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and HOUST does not produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term's rank correlation with returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of an industrial production slump, positive month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates inventory restocking and rising capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months following month-over-month INDPRO growth after a prior 3-month contraction, or the mean excess return in such episodes is negative.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of trigger events, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer inflation exceeding 3% while the federal funds rate remains below 1% creates deeply negative real rates that compress margins and force eventual aggressive tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rate environment with inflation surge
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered weeks, or the interaction term's t-statistic is not significant (|t| < 2) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms above-trend macro momentum that reduces recession probability and supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.5% annualized, or the mean forward return in those quarters does not exceed the unconditional mean.
